title: --runs error message doesn't explain conflict with --min-runs/--max-runs
body: Passing --runs together with --min-runs produces a clap conflict error but it just says the args cannot be used together, without explaining that --runs fixes an exact count while --min-runs/--max-runs bound an adaptive count. Would like a clearer help message.

title: Warmup runs seem to influence the calibration overhead
body: When I pass --warmup 5, the BenchmarkIteration::Warmup variant is used for env var reporting but I'm not sure calibrate() correctly excludes warmup timings from the shell spawning time subtraction. Numbers look off compared to running with --warmup 0.

title: Markdown export doesn't escape pipe characters in command names
body: table_row() joins cells directly with " | " separators. If a benchmarked command itself contains a literal '|' character, the generated markdown table breaks. Please escape or wrap commands in code spans consistently.

title: plot_histogram.py fails on parametrized benchmark JSON
body: Running scripts/plot_histogram.py against a hyperfine --export-json produced by a --parameter-scan run raises a KeyError because the script expects a flat 'results' list without per-parameter grouping.

title: RangeStep should support negative step for descending parameter scans
body: RangeStep<T> in the parameter module only appears to be exercised with positive steps in our tests. Requesting explicit support (and a validation error) for --parameter-scan runs going from a high value down to a low one.
